summary.intent: Address four unresolved Codex review findings on PR #694 in the ars-ui repo, covering RatingGroup and TagGroup components, then push the fixes and re-request review.
summary.outcome: All four Codex findings were fixed (invalid RatingGroup label `for`, step normalization logic, fractional fill attrs, TagGroup post-removal state), full coverage gates passed (98.5% line / 90.1% branch), and the changes were committed and pushed.

repo: Coverage gate is run via `cargo xtask ci coverage` and enforces both line (≥98.5%) and branch (≥90.1%) thresholds for ars-components.
Spec validation is a separate gate: `cargo xtask spec validate`.
`cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject` with a narrow test filter incorrectly flags snapshots from other crates as unreferenced; the full crate-wide insta run must be used for unreferenced checks.
Snapshot acceptance can be done selectively with `cargo insta accept <path>` or globally with `cargo insta accept`.
Codex review threads are queried via GitHub GraphQL API filtering on `isResolved == false` and author login matching 'codex'.

code.finding: RatingGroup label was incorrectly emitting a `for` HTML attribute; removed as invalid for this element type.
Builder path was normalizing step back to 1.0 even when `.step(1.0)` was called explicitly after `.allow_half(true)`; fixed so explicit positive steps take precedence over normalization.
Fractional fill attrs were not being exposed precisely; fixed to pass exact values through.
Post-removal collection and rendering state was implicit; made explicit through the API so callers can observe it deterministically.

workflow: `cargo test -p <crate> --lib <filter1> <filter2>` does not accept two lib-test filters in one invocation; filters must be run as separate commands.
Running `cargo +nightly fmt --all` after code changes is required before clippy and commit; fmt --check is used to verify before committing.
Posting `@codex review` via `gh pr comment` triggers a new Codex review pass; reaction/thread state is polled via GitHub API to detect completion.
The `snapshot-reviewed` label must be reapplied after snapshot changes before re-requesting review.

friction: `cargo test --lib` does not support multiple filter arguments in a single invocation, requiring redundant separate runs.
`cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject` with a narrow test filter produces false positives for snapshots in other crates, making it unusable for focused regression checks.
The step normalization edge case (explicit `.step(1.0)` after `.allow_half(true)`) changed existing test assertions, requiring a snapshot update mid-fix cycle.
Formatting produced a diff after initial code edits, requiring an extra fmt + re-check loop before committing.
open_items: Re-apply `snapshot-reviewed` label after the fix commit before triggering another Codex review pass.
Reply to and resolve the four Codex review threads after pushing.
Trigger another `@codex review` to confirm all findings are addressed.
Monitor CI for the pushed commit to confirm full pipeline green.
